priority_list.description: Remove unsupported H4 causal conclusion. The experiment produced uninformative results (both subsets yield 0.0 faithfulness) because 90% subsetting destroys reconstruction quality — this tests dictionary completeness, not absorption predictive power. The correct experiment (comparing full SAE representations at layers with different absorption profiles, e.g., layer 4 at 49.3% vs layer 8 at 20.9%) was never conducted. No conclusion about 'absorption level predicting circuit importance' can be drawn. Must appear in Abstract, Section 5.3, and Conclusion: 'H4 is inconclusive, not falsified. The correct experiment comparing full SAE representations at layers with different absorption profiles was not conducted.'

Restate H1 hypothesis verdict as layer-dependent. Currently labeled 'falsified' but layer 4 (49.3%) actually CONFIRMS the hypothesis (>20% threshold), while only layer 8 (0.19%) is falsified. Cannot be cleanly labeled as 'falsified' when one layer confirms and another contradicts. Must update Abstract, Section 5.1, and Table 1 to state: 'H1 is falsified at layer 8 (0.19%, <10% threshold) but not at layer 4 (49.3%, >20% threshold). Results show strong layer-dependence requiring explanation.'

Run H2 at layer 4 (49.3% absorption, ~12,000 latents provides ample statistical power) OR formally retire H2 with explicit justification. The correct justification is NOT 'early termination' but 'insufficient absorption variance at layer 8' (46/24,576 latents). However, layer 4 has 260x more data and was never attempted — this failure to execute the pre-registered plan must be acknowledged. Use: 'H2 was not tested at layer 8 due to insufficient variance (46/24,576 latents). Layer 4 (49.3% absorption, ~12,000 latents) was identified as the alternative but not conducted. H2 is formally retired: insufficient statistical power even at the highest-absorption layer tested.'

Investigate the 8 perfect-score latents (A_f=1.0, each firing on exactly 100 tokens). The regularity (100 = number of sequences) strongly suggests positional artifact. Compute token position consistency across sequences for each — if all fire at consistent position X across all 100 sequences, confirm as positional artifact and exclude from primary analysis. Document in Appendix with quantified impact. Do not leave as 'open question' — the pattern is too regular to ignore.

Add mechanistic explanation for H3 inverted-U pattern (absorption peaks at layer 4 with 49.3%, declines to 17.3% at layer 10 despite higher L0 at deeper layers). Current post-hoc speculation ('mid-layers process densest abstract and semantic content') is unsupported by transformer architecture literature. Either: (1) propose a mechanistic hypothesis with citations to layer specialization literature, or (2) explicitly frame the inverted-U as an exploratory finding: 'The inverted-U pattern is an unexpected empirical observation requiring future investigation — we report it as a finding, not a contribution with mechanistic backing.'

Add explicit acknowledgment of predominantly negative results in Abstract and Discussion. Four of five hypotheses failed or were uninformative. Be direct: 'Four of five hypotheses failed or were uninformative. H1 was falsified at layer 8 but confirmed at layer 4. H2 was not tested. H3 was falsified (inverted-U, not monotonic). H4 was inconclusive (both subsets yield 0.0). H5 moved in the hypothesized direction but with negligible practical significance (97.75% non-absorbed even at 2K). The primary contribution is demonstrating that absorption as defined is rare in GPT-2 small, with a validated metric for testing generalization to larger models.'

Add seed ablation (2-3 seeds) for key findings before publication. The dramatic layer discrepancy (layer 4 at 49.3% vs layer 8 at 0.19%) is too large to trust without seed confirmation. If this is a seed artifact, the layer-dependence conclusion is invalid. At minimum, confirm H1 layer comparison and H3 inverted-U across seeds 42, 43, 44. If seed ablation is not feasible before submission, explicitly acknowledge this as a limitation.

Redesign H4 experiment for future work: compare FULL SAE representations at layers with different absorption profiles (layer 4 at 49.3% vs layer 8 at 20.9%), holding dictionary size constant, rather than subsetting latents within a single layer. This would isolate absorption level as the causal variable while preserving dictionary completeness for reconstruction quality. Document this as the correct experiment that was not conducted.

Rephrase H5 framing to use absolute numbers: '97.75% non-absorbed at 2K vs 99.81% at 24K.' The '10-fold reduction' (2.25% to 0.19%) framing overstates practical significance — both dictionary sizes have negligible absorption rates. Consider adding effect size threshold to falsification criterion: a hypothesis moving in the right direction but explaining <1% of variance may warrant 'confirmed_direction_insufficient_magnitude' rather than full 'not falsified.'

Test H4 on multiple circuits (3-5 factual recall prompts with different subject-object pairs), not just France/Paris. Single prompt generalization is insufficient for a circuit-level claim. Use mean faithfulness across prompts as the primary metric.

Global terminology fixes: (1) 'layer-wise' -> 'per-layer' throughout; (2) 'activations' as singular noun -> 'activation'; (3) 'significant' -> 'statistical significance' when referring to p-values. Use global search-and-replace.

Remove all language in Abstract, Section 5.3, and Conclusion that implies H4 shows absorption level does or does not predict circuit importance. Replace with: 'Both absorption subsets yielded 0.0 faithfulness, preventing any conclusion about whether absorption level predicts circuit-level causal importance. The full SAE (0.289) outperforms subsets (0.000), but this tests reconstruction capacity, not absorption quality. The correct experiment comparing full SAE representations at layers with different absorption profiles was not conducted.'

Fix Section 6.4 percentage statement: 'Approximately 79% of latents' is derived from 100 - 20.9 = 79.1 but conflates distinct categories. Replace with explicit decomposition: 'At layer 8, 20.9% of latents show A_f > 0.5 (absorbed), 76.8% show A_f = 0.0 (completely independent), and 2.3% show 0 < A_f <= 0.5 (partial absorption). The two clean categories account for 97.7% of latents.'

Fix figure reference in Section 5.2: 'Figure 2 shows the detailed absorption score distribution for layer 4' should reference Figure 4. Figure 2 (fig1_layer_absorption.pdf) shows the inverted-U across all 6 layers.

Change 'shows that' to 'suggests that' in Section 5.3 and Section 6.2 when referring to the dictionary completeness interpretation. The evidence is consistent with this interpretation but does not definitively prove it given the design flaw.
